title: Buchnera forbesii
taxonomy: Buchnera forbesii is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Phrymaceae and the genus Buchnera. The species Buchnera forbesii is native to North America. It belongs to the order Lamiales and is a flowering plant. More specifically, it is classified as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Family - Phrymaceae, Genus - Buchnera, and Species - B. forbesii. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the same family and genus.

intro: Buchnera forbesii is a plant species native to India, belonging to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The bioactive constituents of Buchnera forbesii include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
